Steps.Message:
Hi, it's Chev from the messaging team at UK Power Networks. I understand how difficult it can be without power and the additional worries this can bring.

For both our business customers and those at home, we will work as quickly and safely as possible to restore supplies. If you need any additional support or have any concerns, please call us on 105 or tweet us @ukpowernetworks.

I will personally continue to keep you updated with our progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers have investigated and found that the power is off due to another company working in the area causing damage to our underground network. This has affected our substation equipment in the Annison Street TN9 area.

Although we know what's causing your power cut, we still need to locate the fault. To do this we're sending a specialist team.

Our engineers have let us know that to give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 15:00 and 16:00. Sent by Chev at 11:25.

Sent by Lee at 21:53 - I'm texting to say I am glad to report that power to the Lyndale Road, RH1 2 area should now be back on.

I'm sorry for the continued disruption recently. We believe there may be a fault on our underground electricity cabling in the area, but currently it has not developed to a stage that our staff can pinpoint it. Their tests have proven inconclusive, so for now they have replaced our safety equipment at the substation to get your power back on as quickly as possible.

This may happen again before we're able to fix it, but if you'd like to talk to us about this, or if your power isn't on call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Decasta at 01:26 - Our engineers have restored power to all 3332 properties in the South Ockendon RM15  area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around an underground cabling fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.

Sent by Decasta at 01:03 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an underground cabling fault that initially interrupted power to 3332 properties.

However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 290 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment.

We're currently estimating power will be on between 02:30-03:30, but this may change depending on what our staff find.

Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.
